chorus, and sometimes a precourse. But
don't let that name fool you. It's not
only pop songs that use this system of a
down does it. Lincoln Park, Metallica,

easy way to think about it is verses ask
questions and choruses answer them.
There's sort of a call-in response
between verses and choruses. In the
first verse, you set the stage
highlighting the situation, the problem,
the story, whatever it is. And then the
chorus is like the main idea, sort of
bringing this all together. And then the
second verse can either restate the
first verse in a different way or it can
move the story forward. So, the easiest
way to add structure to your songs is by
using meta tags, which I'm sure you've

examples of how song structure is done
is to just look at any of your

pop structure. What? So, the way this

different song structures are going to
give your song a different flow of

where there isn't a typical verse,

movie where it kind of goes up in

then there's something intense at the
end that feels kind of like a chorus.
